backstory: Mid-level engineer at a 50-person Series B company. Working in a turbo-managed monorepo. Sentry already deployed company-wide. Wants tools that help *them* personally, not the org.

repo_signals.stack: turbo monorepo, next.js + nest.js + postgres + redis

expected_wow: The digest surfaces personal-scope items: 'PRs #1421, #1438 still open, you wrote both'. Stale-PR nudger is the personalized wow — they get to look like a 10x by closing their open PRs before standup.
expected_dropoff_risk: Org policy on third-party connects (turn 4). They might say 'I can't connect company GitHub' — the GitHub-App per-repo scoping should help here (only watches one repo, not the whole org). Connect step needs to surface that scoping explicitly so the user feels safe.
wow_score_target: ≥ 2 in heuristic 5 (signal-to-suggestion specificity — they'll dismiss generic suggestions) and at least H6 = 1 (wow is digest, not earlier)
scoring_notes: This persona has the most *infra already in place*. Recommend.md output (if reached) will be thin because Sentry+dependabot+CI cover most slots. The wow has to be the digest itself, not gap-filling. If the digest's chief-of-staff briefing names specific PRs by number, scores 3 on H5.
